mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets that boost consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 8.0%, or the signal's predictive Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and new housing starts indicates broad-based economic expansion that lifts corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing, or forward returns turn negative when both proxies show simultaneous month-over-month declines.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and supports equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain flat or negative during sustained INDPRO uptrends, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.01 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An abrupt increase in the federal funds rate compresses equity risk premiums and triggers portfolio rebalancing away from duration-sensitive assets, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in over 55% of triggered instances, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% across a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail and food sales data signals robust consumer demand that directly translates into higher corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns show no statistically significant correlation with RSAFS growth rates, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during periods of high inflation volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with stable consumer price inflation confirm a non-inflationary labor market recovery, which sustains accommodative monetary policy expectations and lifts equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns when PAYEMS rises but CPIAUCSL increases by more than 0.3% month-over-month, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts during a late-cycle expansion phase indicate peak consumer confidence and credit availability, which temporarily boosts cyclical equity valuations before mean reversion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when HOUST growth exceeds the 12-month moving average by more than 15%, or the signal's Sharpe ratio falls below zero over a 3-year rolling window.
